name: Hungarian Beef Stew
recipeIngredient: 1 pound beef suitable for stewing (i.e., chuck, round), cut into bite-size pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the cooking oil in a large heavy pot with a cover.
2. Add onions and cook covered over medium-low heat for ten minutes, stirring occasionally. The onions should be translucent and limp after that time, but not brown.
3. Remove the onions (invert the covered pot to catch them) and set aside.
4. Raise the heat and brown the beef cubes in batches, so as not to cool the pot by adding too many at once. Return the onions; add tomato paste and seasonings, and just enough water to barely cover the mixture.
5. Stir well, then raise the heat to bring the pot to a boil, and reduce the heat to a simmer.
6. Cook for as long as you can manage, at least three hours, or longer in an oven heated to 250 degrees Fahrenheit. The onions should dissolve and disappear into the sauce. The longer it cooks over low heat, the better it will taste, and it's even better the day after it's first cooked.
7. Serve ladled over cooked noodles or rice, and use any leftovers as a shepherd's pie topped with mashed potato mixed with egg and browned in the oven.
